content.problemStatement:
Given a binary string SSS, we can perform the following operation as many times as we want:


Choose iii such that 1‚Č§i<‚ą£S‚ą£1 \le i < |S|1‚Č§i<‚ą£S‚ą£ and Si‚Č†Si+1S_i \ne S_{i + 1}Si‚ÄčÓÄ†=Si+1‚Äč, and either delete SiS_iSi‚Äč or Si+1S_{i + 1}Si+1‚Äč from the string (concatenate the remaining parts of the string).
Find the minimum final length of the string SSS after performing some (possibly 000) number of operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the length of the string
The second line contains SSS - a binary string




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum final length

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Perform the following operations:

Initially S=1010S = 1010S=1010
Pick i=1i = 1i=1, verify S1‚Č†S2S_1 \ne S_2S1‚ÄčÓÄ†=S2‚Äč, and then we choose to delete S1S_1S1‚Äč. The new SSS is 010010010.
Pick i=2i = 2i=2, verify S2‚Č†S3S_2 \ne S_3S2‚ÄčÓÄ†=S3‚Äč, and then we choose to delete S3S_3S3‚Äč. The new SSS is 010101.
Pick i=1i = 1i=1, verify S1‚Č†S2S_1 \ne S_2S1‚ÄčÓÄ†=S2‚Äč, and then we choose to delete S1S_1S1‚Äč. The new SSS is 111.

Thus, the final string length is 111.
